name: Cosmos Network
northStar: Deep space command center — a near-black financial observatory where white type and concentric diagrams float in measured silence.

dos: Use 'The Future' exclusively at weight 400 — the system carries authority through spacing and restraint, not bold weights
Maintain 20px border-radius for all interactive surfaces (buttons, nav items, cards) to preserve the pill geometry
Keep the canvas near-pure-black (#000000) with #1e1f20 as the only card elevation — avoid introducing mid-gray fills that dilute contrast
Apply positive letter-spacing from the type scale to every text element, including body copy at small sizes
Use #ffffff for all text and icon strokes; introduce #807f7f only for genuine de-emphasis, never for decoration
Anchor hero and section layouts on a two-column split (text-left, visual-right) with the headline at 60px display size
Reserve #22e2a8 for live-status or active-state indicators only — do not use it as a brand color or button fill
donts: Do not introduce a second typeface — 'The Future' at varying sizes and tracking covers the entire system
Do not use colored backgrounds (blue, teal, orange) as section backgrounds — these appeared only in decorative diagrams and would break the monochrome system
Do not add box-shadows or glows to cards or buttons — the system relies on flat dark surfaces and hairline borders, never elevation effects
Do not bold or italicize type — weight 400 with tracking is the only typographic move available
Do not place buttons in a filled-white style on dark sections — the ghost outlined pill is the canonical action surface
Do not use #0000ee or any default-link blue — links inherit #ffffff with optional underline only
Do not introduce gradients between surfaces — the system is strictly flat, with color appearing only in infographic elements

colors.name: Void Black
colors.role: Page canvas, section backgrounds, maximum-contrast surface

colors.name: Carbon
colors.role: Navigation bar, elevated surface base, footer surface

colors.name: Graphite
colors.role: Card surfaces, elevated panels, contained content blocks

colors.name: Slate
colors.role: Muted secondary text, inactive nav items, subtle metadata

colors.name: Fog
colors.role: Disabled labels, low-emphasis helper text, tertiary metadata

colors.name: Chalk
colors.role: Alternate light surface, inverted card context

colors.name: Pure White
colors.role: Primary headings, body text, icon strokes, button borders, link text — the single chromatic force in the system

colors.name: Signal Mint
colors.role: Green wash for highlight backgrounds, decorative bands, and soft emphasis behind content

colors.name: Deep Navy
colors.role: Ambient atmospheric tint, decorative background wash behind globe visualization

colors.name: Concentric Teal
colors.role: Diagram and data-visualization stroke, subtle infographic accent

designSystem.layout: Page architecture is centered max-width contained at ~1280px with generous outer padding. The hero is a two-column split: headline + body left (~55%), globe diagram right (~45%), with the headline at 60px and a clear vertical hierarchy of headline → body → CTA → stat strip below. Below the hero, sections flow seamlessly on the same #000000 canvas with 80px vertical gaps between blocks. Case study content uses a horizontal card carousel with 4 visible cards and partial bleed on the right edge to suggest scroll continuation, with navigation dots top-right. Navigation is a single horizontal bar — no sidebar, no mega-menu overlay visible. The overall rhythm is quiet and spacious: large headlines anchor dark sections, whitespace separates content blocks, and no section uses a contrasting background color, keeping the monochrome system intact throughout.
designSystem.imagery: Visual language is restrained and diagrammatic rather than photographic. The hero uses a single large data-visualization element — a dark globe render with concentric circle nodes and white annotation labels (FIGURE, Ondo fintech, ripple) that reads like a schematic or system diagram. Case study cards use real photography but crop it tight to the top of the card, desaturated to fit the dark palette, functioning as content headers rather than lifestyle imagery. No full-bleed editorial photography exists anywhere on the page. Iconography is minimal-stroke white line icons used sparingly for nav dropdowns and directional arrows. The overall visual register is institutional and technical — closer to an annual report or infrastructure dashboard than a consumer product.

similar.why: Same dark-canvas financial-infrastructure aesthetic with white tracked type and minimal color
Large declarative headlines on near-black backgrounds with pill-shaped ghost controls and flat card surfaces
Institutional crypto research aesthetic with dark backgrounds, generous whitespace, and single-weight geometric type
Financial trust language expressed through monochrome dark surfaces and measured typography rather than color

surfaces.name: Carbon
surfaces.purpose: Navigation bar, footer, primary surface container

surfaces.name: Graphite
surfaces.purpose: Elevated card surfaces, content blocks above canvas

surfaces.name: Chalk
surfaces.purpose: Inverted light surface for contrast sections or modal dialogs

designSystem.northStar: Deep space command center — a near-black financial observatory where white type and concentric diagrams float in measured silence.

components.name: Ghost Pill Button
components.role: Primary interactive control used for top-level calls-to-action in navigation and hero
components.description: Transparent fill with 1px white border (#ffffff), 20px border-radius (pill shape), 10px 30px padding, text in 'The Future' at 16px weight 400, letter-spacing 0.025em, with a trailing arrow icon ›

components.name: Card Surface
components.role: Container for case studies, features, and content blocks
components.description: 20px border-radius, #1e1f20 background, no visible border or shadow, generous internal padding (20px), full-bleed image at top with sharp top corners rounding to match card

components.name: Case Study Card
components.role: Horizontal card displaying a customer/partnership story in a scrollable carousel
components.description: Image header (rounded top corners), #1e1f20 background, 20px radius, white company name at 24px with positive tracking, body description at 16px in #807f7f, small uppercase category tag at bottom (Lending, Payments, Tokenization)

components.name: Navigation Bar
components.description: Dark Carbon (#181818) background, 'C☿SMOS' wordmark left, four nav items centered (Solutions, Technology, Resources, About) with dropdown indicators, ghost pill CTA 'Contact Us' right-aligned

components.name: Headline Display Block
components.description: Left-aligned, 'The Future' at 60px weight 400, pure white (#ffffff), line-height 1.13, letter-spacing 0.04em, occupies left half of a two-column hero layout

components.name: Body Paragraph
components.description: 'The Future' at 16px weight 400, white with reduced visual weight, line-height 1.6, max-width ~540px, left-aligned in hero context

components.name: Stat Strip
components.role: Horizontal row of key metrics in the hero
components.description: Single line, 'The Future' at 24–32px, large numeric value in white followed by descriptive label in #807f7f, items separated by generous horizontal spacing (30px+)

components.name: Carousel Navigation Dots
components.role: Indicator for horizontal scroll position in case-study carousel
components.description: Small circular dots (6px), #807f7f for inactive, #ffffff for active state, positioned top-right of carousel header

components.name: Globe Visualization
components.description: Stylized Earth render in dark tones with concentric circles radiating from a 'TDeFi' node, white annotation labels ('FIGURE', 'Ondo fintech', 'ripple'), positioned right of headline

components.name: Footer Surface
components.role: Site footer with links and metadata
components.description: #181818 background, same nav and typography as header, hairline #333333 top border, compact link rows

components.name: Category Tag Label
components.role: Small uppercase tag at bottom of case study cards
components.description: 'The Future' at 12px weight 400, letter-spacing 0.02em, #807f7f color, no background or border, sits flush left at card bottom

typography.role: Sole typeface across the entire system — navigation, body, headings, buttons, and labels. The slight positive tracking (0.02–0.04em) at all sizes creates a measured, architectural quality uncommon in fintech.
typography.substitute: Geist, Inter, or DM Sans with positive letter-spacing applied at small sizes

designSystem.description: Cosmos Network presents an institutional dark-mode financial infrastructure aesthetic: a near-black canvas where white typography does all the visual work, supported by pill-shaped ghost controls and generous whitespace. The single custom typeface 'The Future' carries a slight positive tracking that gives headlines a measured, ceremonial quality — authority through restraint rather than volume. Layout follows a disciplined two-column rhythm: large declarative headlines paired with restrained secondary imagery (like the concentric-circle globe diagram), followed by horizontal case-study card carousels. Color is almost entirely suppressed — any chromatic presence is deeply desaturated, creating a quiet, high-trust environment where structure and whitespace carry the weight.

- text: #ffffff
- background: #000000
- border: #333333
- muted text: #807f7f
- card surface: #1e1f20
- nav surface: #181818
- primary action: no distinct CTA color

## Example Component Prompts

1. **Hero section (dark, two-column)**: #000000 canvas, max-width 1280px centered. Left column: 60px 'The Future' weight 400, #ffffff, letter-spacing 2.4px, headline text. Below: 16px body, #807f7f, line-height 1.6, max-width 540px. Below: ghost pill button, 1px #ffffff border, 20px radius, 10px 30px padding, 16px 'The Future' #ffffff with arrow. Right column: 400px globe diagram with concentric circles, white annotation labels.

2. **Case Study Card**: #1e1f20 background, 20px radius, 20px internal padding. Top: full-width image header, 16:9 ratio, top corners rounded to 20px. Middle: 24px 'The Future' weight 400, #ffffff, company name. Below: 16px 'The Future', #807f7f, 2-line description. Bottom: 12px 'The Future', #807f7f, letter-spacing 0.24px, category tag.

3. **Navigation Bar**: #181818 background, full-width, 80px height. Left: 'C☿SMOS' wordmark in 16px 'The Future' weight 400, #ffffff. Center: four nav items at 16px 'The Future', #ffffff, separated by 30px gap, each with a dropdown chevron icon. Right: ghost pill button 'Contact Us' with 1px #ffffff border, 20px radius, 10px 30px padding.

4. **Stat Strip**: horizontal row, 24px 'The Future' weight 400, #ffffff for numeric values (150+, $70bn, 10 years+, 10000+, <1 second), followed immediately by 16px 'The Future', #807f7f, for descriptor text. Items separated by 30px gap, no dividers.

5. **Carousel Header with Nav Dots**: 36px 'The Future' weight 400, #ffffff headline on the left. Right side: two small 6px circular dots, #807f7f (inactive) and #ffffff (active), 8px gap between them, positioned vertically centered with the headline.

customSections.title: Typographic System Philosophy
customSections.content: The system uses a single weight (400) across all roles — the visual hierarchy is created entirely through size, tracking, and color opacity. Letter-spacing scales proportionally: smaller text gets tighter positive tracking (0.02em at 12px) while display text gets expansive tracking (0.04em at 60px). This creates a consistent 'architectural' feel where every element feels carefully measured. The font features ss02 and ss09 should always be enabled to preserve the intended character variants. Never simulate bold with font-weight — use size and tracking instead.

designSystem.elevationPhilosophy: The system intentionally avoids all shadow and glow effects. Elevation is communicated exclusively through surface tone shifts: #000000 canvas → #181818 nav/footer → #1e1f20 card. No drop-shadows, no inner-shadows, no blur effects. This flat elevation strategy creates a clean, diagrammatic quality where every element sits clearly on the same plane, reinforcing the institutional and technical character of the brand.
